title: Warm Hearty French Corned Beef Pot au Feu
description: A savory, cheese-pull crowd-pleaser featuring corned beef, wok-hei, and dairy-free-friendly options. This classic French dish is perfect for a special occasion or a cozy night in.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the sliced onions and cook until they're translucent and caramelized, about 20 minutes.
Add the sliced carrots and celery and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the corned beef brisket and brown it on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the beef broth, red wine, tomato paste, and thyme to the pot. Bring the liquid to a boil, then cover the pot and transfer it to the oven.
Bake the pot au feu for 2 hours, or until the meat is tender and the vegetables are cooked through.
Remove the pot from the oven and carefully remove the meat from the pot. Slice it thinly against the grain.
Return the pot to the stovetop and bring the liquid to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the pot au feu simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Serve the sliced meat and vegetables hot, topped with grated Gruyère cheese (or nutritional yeast).
Garnish with fresh thyme and serve immediately.
